item.title: Shibboleth: The Tech
item.description: In my last post I said that I wrote a “a simple Reddit bot to crawl through users’ post histories on demand, letting me know when and where they’re using far right shibboleths.” Technically speaking, that’s not 100% true. My robot is actually just a script running every ten minutes that parses a user’s 1000 most recent comments. PRAW The Python Reddit API Wrapper is, as the name says, a Python wrapper for the Reddit API.

item.title: Shibboleth
item.description: The Gileadites captured the fords of the Jordan leading to Ephraim, and whenever a survivor of Ephraim said, “Let me cross over,” the men of Gilead asked him, “Are you an Ephraimite?” If he replied, “No,” they said, “All right, say ‘Shibboleth.’” If he said, “Sibboleth,” because he could not pronounce the word correctly, they seized him and killed him at the fords of the Jordan. Forty-two thousand Ephraimites were killed at that time.

item.title: Lesson: Elevator Security
item.description: During the nicer months in Montreal, I bike to work. We recently moved to a new building that has a parking garage below it, and a bike rack in the parking garage. To get to my bike after work I: Take an elevator down to the basement Use my key fob to open a locked door Take a second elevator down a level Between steps 2 and 3 something really nice happens.

item.title: Inspiration
item.description: Design that has made a positive impact on my life. No matter how small.

item.title: Arriving without dying.
item.description: How can I get somewhere I’ve never been, while not negatively impacting my safety as a cyclist? I try to be a safe biker. I wear my helmet. I use lights. I don’t look at my phone. I never, ever wear headphones. I realize that biking, even in a city with as much bike infrastructure as Montreal, has some inherent dangers, so I do my best to make sure that I’m not limiting myself by blocking off what I’m hearing.

item.title: About
item.description: I have many problems. This is where I’ll document my attempts to solve all of them with computers.

item.title: New Blog
item.description: Garbage I’ve been terrible at personal projects, and especially terrible at keeping them somewhere that other people can see them. I’m looking to change that this year. To start, I’ll be detailing my current project in a few posts over the coming weeks, and I’m going to start doing regular updates on whatever projects I have ongoing. This blog should at least serve as a decent dev-diary.
